revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Fuse Kofun]][[ja:布勢古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Fuse Kofun | native_name =布勢古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Map of Tottori Prefecture Ja.svg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Fuse Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.50527777777778|N|134.1772222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tottori (city)|en|Tottori (city)|qid=Q200731|lt=Tottori (city)|1=Tottori (city)}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = no facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|19|N|134|10|38|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Fuse Kofun'''|布勢古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Fuse neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tottori (city)|en|Tottori (city)|qid=Q200731|lt=Tottori|1=Tottori (city)}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172825|title= 布勢古墳|trans-title= Fuse Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Monument== The Fuse area of Tottori city, in the vicinity of Lake Koyama in the western part of the Tottori Plain, contains many ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including a place where {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} bronze ''[[:en:Dotaku|Dotaku]]'' have been discovered. The Fuse Kofun is located on the east bank of Lake Koyama, on a hill with an elevation of approximately 30 meters from its surroundings. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown. Fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, and the shape of the tumulus, date it to the late Kofun period, or the first half of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The mound is a keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} type of ''[[kofun]]'', orientated to the west. It is one of the larger tumuli is eastern Tottori Prefecture, with a total length of about 60 meters; the circular portion is approximately 29 meters in diameter and 5 meters high, and the rectangular portion is approximately 21 meters wide. No moat has been detected.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|qid=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F326DBBD001CA0E14925796F0007FCE4?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.toritabi.net/tottori/fusekofu.html Tottori Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
